title: Add feedback button using noos feedback widget
description: Add a feedback/suggestion button to the site that uses the noos feedback widget. This lets visitors leave feedback about the site, suggest features, or report issues.

title: Add featured conversations section to gallery
description: Add a dedicated 'Featured' section at the top of the gallery page that highlights curated conversations. Featured conversations should appear in a visually distinct section above the main list with larger cards or a different layout. The featured flag already exists in the data model (featured: true on seed conversations) — this ticket is about surfacing those in the UI as a separate section rather than just a badge inline.

title: Add authors/contributors page linked from top nav
description:
Add an 'Authors' or 'Contributors' link in the top navigation bar that leads to a dedicated page (or section) listing all contributors. Each author entry could show: name, number of conversations, and link to their filtered gallery view. The contributor filter pills on the gallery already work — this ticket is about making authors discoverable from the nav and giving them a proper page/profile.

ALSO: On the gallery cards and reader view, the author name should be clickable — clicking it filters the gallery to that author (or navigates to their author page). On hover over a card, the author name could show a tooltip or become a distinct clickable element that pivots to their conversations without opening the chat itself.

title: Add highlight/excerpt section to each conversation
description: Every conversation should have a 'highlight' section — a curated excerpt or pull-quote that captures the most striking moment. Could be: (1) A single standout exchange shown at the top of the reader before the full conversation. (2) A highlighted passage marked by the uploader or curator. (3) The excerpt shown on the gallery card. This helps visitors quickly understand why a conversation is worth reading.

title: Toggle to collapse AI responses, showing only human messages
description: Add a toggle in the reader view that collapses all AI responses, showing only the human messages. This lets you quickly scan what questions/prompts were asked and decide whether to read the full conversation. Inspired by David Bieber's feedback. Could be: (1) A toggle button in the reader header ('Show prompts only' / 'Show full conversation'). (2) AI messages collapse to a single line preview with click-to-expand. (3) Could also work on the gallery level for browsing.

title: Simplify topic tags UX — too many tags is overwhelming
description: The tag filter section currently shows all tags from all conversations, which creates a wall of pills (20+). Ideas to fix: (1) Collapse tags behind a 'More topics' toggle, showing only top 5-8 by frequency. (2) Group related tags (e.g. 'game-theory', 'poker', 'diplomacy' under 'Strategy'). (3) Use a search/autocomplete instead of pills. (4) Only show tags after a contributor is selected. The goal is to reduce visual noise while keeping discoverability.
close_reason: Implemented: tags collapse behind '+ N more' button, showing only top 6 by default
